evidence_register.note: Curated explicit Mike/Rich decisions on employed doctors, lead ownership, and the post-close wire sequence through signed contract, payment confirmation, and PayMe posting. Tentative fit, criminal-history, political, religious, rescheduling-exception, and distribution discussion remains non-answering discovery evidence.
Curated the explicit Call 1 pricing rule and Rich's three-month main-ISTV reapplication minimum. Rich controls the same decision over later conflicting Sales Ops replies.
No duplicate policies added. Existing independently verified Slack records already cover no DJ cohort, no DJ discounts, and use of approved payment options.
Mike explicitly approved the complete displayed answer. The source sequence places this approval after the Thursday July 9 marker; chronology is recorded conservatively at the start of July 9.
Higher-resolution duplicate of the answer Mike approved; used to preserve every condition without adding a duplicate policy.
Curated Rich's exact correction for supported ISTV app devices and the unresolved-question route.

The automated classifier captured the contract-term boundary from this thread but omitted Madeline's separate instruction to use the new upgrade form before selecting the contract. The omitted atomic decision is curated without changing the source thread.

Curated only Madeline's explicit operational instruction to assist a first-time Zoom user with joining. Her tentative red-flag commentary remains non-answering and cannot be used to decide fit or qualification.

Madeline's reply answered both parts of the exact repeated-disqualified-applicant scenario. The compiled summary retained the tech/opt-out process but omitted the explicit permission to cancel, so the complete bounded decision is curated here.

Rich's direct reply resolves the Friday Call 1 scenario: weekend work is optional and Call 2 may be scheduled Monday. The generated summaries blurred which call moves to Monday, so this curated decision restores the source thread's exact Call 1 and Call 2 roles.
Madeline confirmed that an additional attendee may use the same onboarding link.
Madeline directed Call 1 greenlight-supporting documents and links to the fulfillment hotline.
Madeline supplied bounded definitions for SEO, social promotional assets, and swag.
Madeline clarified that Daymond creates a small license-dependent custom intro and does not personally conduct the studio interview.
Madeline explicitly distinguished documentary-style programming from reality TV.
Madeline defined second calls as follow-ups, directed corrections to the original EOD thread, and routed live leaderboard corrections to Tech with Alex tagged.
Madeline later confirmed that sending the direct Zoom link is acceptable for the immediate booking case.
Madeline later confirmed that the rep may include the public OnceHub link in the first no-answer text.
Madeline confirmed that three partners from one business may appear together in one episode and should participate together.
The authoritative reply permits calling the same outbound lead twice in one day and says that placing the rep's name on the outbound sheet marks the lead as taken and already being contacted.
The authoritative reply permits calling an outbound lead whose Keap assignment displays Rudy Mawer.
Madeline allowed telephone payment and onboarding for the no-internet case while requiring Zoom video when possible and approving the Zoom-recorded phone workaround.
Madeline denied a Monday exception for a bank-closure payment miss. The attached six-month wait is replaced by Rich's controlling three-month normal minimum.
Madeline permitted a bounded follow-up attempt after a pre-call cancellation when the lead has not said do not contact.
Madeline confirmed that the completed greenlight stays in the original day's stats and approved reconnecting and booking a follow-up after the internet drop.
Madeline approved the complete wire SOP: sign the contract on Zoom, obtain wire proof promptly, verify receipt with Alex in 24 to 48 hours, and wait for confirmation before PayMe posting.

policies.title: America's Best Doctors does not require practice ownership
question_families: Can an employed doctor qualify for America's Best Doctors?
Does an America's Best Doctors applicant have to own a medical practice?
Can a doctor who works at a hospital be cast without owning the practice?
policies.decision: An employed doctor can qualify for America's Best Doctors even if they work in a hospital and do not own the practice. Owning a medical practice or business is not a hard requirement. Boundaries: This decision does not resolve whether nurses, physical therapists, or other non-doctor medical professionals qualify; route those profession-boundary cases for current approval.
policies.search_text: America's Best Doctors employed doctor hospital own medical practice business ownership required qualify greenlight fit An employed doctor can qualify even without owning the practice

policies.title: Call 1 pricing discussion boundary
question_families: Can a rep discuss price on Call 1?
When is pricing allowed on the first sales call?
Can I mention the price to disqualify someone who has no business and cannot afford it?
policies.decision: Do not mention price on Call 1 by default. A rep may mention it only when the rep is sure the client does not have a business and is not financially qualified, for the purpose of disqualifying that client. Conditions: The rep is sure both that the client has no business and that the client is not financially qualified. Boundaries: This does not authorize routine Call 1 price disclosure or disclosure based on only one of those conditions.
policies.search_text: Call 1 first call price pricing discuss mention disqualify no business not financially qualified default do not disclose pricing

policies.title: Lead ownership lasts 30 days from latest documented contact
question_families: How long does a rep own a lead after contacting them?
Does the 30-day lead ownership window refresh after another call?
If I log a real contact today, when does my 30-day lead ownership expire?
policies.decision: A rep owns a lead for 30 days from the rep's latest contact with that lead when the contact is documented in Keap. A later documented contact restarts the 30-day window. Conditions: The contact is documented in Keap. Boundaries: This does not replace the separate first-booking rule when a client is simultaneously booked with multiple reps.
policies.search_text: lead ownership 30 days thirty days latest contact documented communication Keap refresh restart expiration outbound

policies.title: Same-day onboarding needs no separate notification after all post-sale steps
question_families: Do I need to notify anyone when I book a same-day onboarding call?
A client just closed and booked onboarding today; do I need to message the team?
Will the studio executive team be ready for a short-notice onboarding booking?
policies.decision: No separate notification is required for a same-day onboarding call when all required post-sale steps are complete: payment has been taken, the contract is signed, the onboarding email has been reviewed and sent, and the available onboarding call has been booked. The studio executive team handles the onboarding call and will be ready. Conditions: Every listed post-sale step is complete and an available same-day slot was booked. Boundaries: If any listed step is incomplete or the issue is a post-sale change rather than the initial booking, use the normal fulfillment route.
policies.route_reason: Route incomplete or changed post-sale cases to fulfillment.
policies.search_text: same day short notice onboarding notify message fulfillment studio executive team payment contract signed onboarding email post sale steps ready

policies.title: Use the new upgrade form before selecting VIP upgrade terms
question_families: What is the first step when a client wants to upgrade to VIP?
Should I use the new upgrade form before choosing the upgrade contract?
How do I start a VIP upgrade when the client requests different payment terms?
policies.decision: Use the new upgrade form to fill out the client's upgrade information before selecting or sending the contract and payment option. Boundaries: This instruction does not authorize a custom payment plan, identify the exact current form link, or permit edits to contract terms.
policies.search_text: VIP upgrade new upgrade form fill out information first step before choose select send contract payment option six month plan

policies.title: Where the ISTV app can be downloaded
question_families: Which TV devices support the ISTV app?
Where can someone download and watch the ISTV app?
Can prospects watch ISTV on Roku, Fire Stick, or Apple TV?
policies.decision: The ISTV app can be downloaded on Roku, Fire Stick, or Apple TV devices. If the question remains unresolved, use #sales-questions-requests. Boundaries: This identifies supported device families; it does not guarantee availability for every region, model, or current app-store status.
policies.route_reason: Use the sales questions channel if the supported-device answer does not resolve the request.
policies.search_text: ISTV app download watch stream supported devices Roku Fire Stick Apple TV sales questions requests unresolved

policies.title: Assist a first-time Zoom user with joining the meeting
question_families: How should a rep help an applicant who is using Zoom for the first time?
What should I do when an applicant is struggling to join the Zoom meeting?
Should a Zoom-unfamiliarity no-show be handled only as a generic no-show?
policies.decision: Try your best to assist an applicant who is unfamiliar with Zoom by walking them through how to join the meeting. Boundaries: Zoom difficulty alone does not establish whether the applicant is a good or bad fit, does not authorize disqualification, and does not replace a separate qualification assessment.
policies.route_reason: Route only an unresolved technical failure; do not route or decide fit merely because the applicant needed first-time Zoom help.
policies.search_text: first time Zoom unfamiliar difficulty struggling join meeting applicant late no show assist walk through how to join do not decide fit

policies.title: Cancel and block a repeatedly disqualified applicant who keeps reapplying
question_families: Can I cancel an audition for someone I recently disqualified who keeps applying again?
How should a rep stop repeat bookings from a repeatedly disqualified applicant?
What should I do when the same unsuitable applicant repeatedly returns to the pipeline?
policies.decision: A rep may cancel the audition when a repeatedly disqualified applicant keeps applying again. Post the case in #sales-tech-requests to ask for the applicant to be unsubscribed or blocked from reapplying, and add the applicant's information to the official opt-out process. Conditions: The applicant has been disqualified repeatedly and keeps reapplying. Boundaries: This does not authorize automatic cancellation for every prior pass or single reapplication; those cases require the applicable ownership and waiting-period process.
policies.route_reason: Use sales tech to implement the unsubscribe or block request.
policies.search_text: repeatedly disqualified applicant keeps applying again repeat bookings cancel audition sales tech unsubscribe block reapplying opt out pipeline unsuitable applicant

policies.title: Friday Call 1 allows Call 2 on Monday without mandatory weekend work
question_families: If Call 1 happens Friday, may Call 2 be scheduled Monday?
Does a Friday Call 1 require the rep to conduct Call 2 before the Sunday cohort closes?
Must a rep work the weekend after a Friday Call 1?
policies.decision: When Call 1 happens on Friday, the rep is not required to work over the weekend. If the rep does not conduct Call 2 on Saturday or Sunday, Call 2 may be scheduled for Monday. Boundaries: This resolves the Friday Call 1 and following Call 2 scheduling choice; it does not establish a different general cohort deadline.
policies.search_text: Friday Call 1 Call 2 Monday Saturday Sunday weekend optional cohort scheduling no mandatory weekend work

policies.title: Main ISTV reapplication minimum is three months
question_families: How long must a main ISTV prospect wait before reapplying after a no-show?
What is the reapplication wait after missing the Call 2 payment or signature deadline?
When may a rejected or not-fit main ISTV prospect reapply?
policies.decision: For main ISTV, a prospect who no-shows, misses the applicable Call 2 payment or signature deadline, or is rejected or found not fit may be told they can reapply in the future. The minimum waiting period is three months. Boundaries: This normal waiting-period rule does not replace a separately approved documented exception or the repeated-abuse blocking process.
policies.search_text: main ISTV reapply reapplication no show Call 1 Call 2 greenlit missed Sunday payment signature deadline rejected not fit wait minimum three months 90 days

policies.title: An additional attendee may use the same onboarding link
question_families: May a client's family member join onboarding with the same link?
Can an additional attendee use the client's onboarding link?
policies.decision: Yes. An additional attendee may join the onboarding call using the same onboarding link as the client.
policies.search_text: onboarding additional attendee mother family member same onboarding link share link join call

policies.title: Send lead-provided greenlight evidence to the fulfillment hotline
question_families: Where should I send documents a Call 1 lead provides for greenlight approval?
Which channel receives links supporting a prospect's greenlight decision?
policies.decision: Send documents or links supplied by a Call 1 lead to support greenlight approval to the fulfillment hotline.
policies.route_reason: The stable destination itself answers this workflow question.
policies.search_text: greenlight supporting documents links Call 1 lead evidence approval fulfillment hotline send where channel

policies.title: SEO helps the client or business rank higher on Google
question_families: What does SEO mean in our package?
What does the SEO service do for the client?
policies.decision: SEO is intended to help the client or the client's business rank higher on Google for affiliated keywords.
policies.search_text: SEO search engine optimization rank higher Google affiliated keywords client business package meaning definition

policies.title: Social promotional assets announce the network feature
question_families: What are social promotional assets?
What does social promo assets mean in the offer?
policies.decision: Social promotional assets are content created for the client's social media or website to announce the client's feature on the network. Boundaries: This definition does not promise ongoing social-media management or identify every asset in a selected package.
policies.search_text: social promotional assets content social media website announce network feature meaning definition package deliverable not management

policies.title: Swag means promotional merchandise or souvenir material
question_families: What does swag mean in the package?
What is the swag deliverable?
policies.decision: Swag means merchandise, a souvenir, or promotional material.
policies.search_text: swag merchandise souvenir promotional material package deliverable meaning definition

policies.title: Daymond creates a custom intro but does not conduct the studio interview
question_families: Does Daymond personally interview Next Level CEO clients?
What does Daymond personally create for the client's episode?
policies.decision: Daymond does not personally interview Next Level CEO clients in the studio. He creates a small custom intro, and the exact intro depends on the license the client purchases.
policies.search_text: Daymond John personally interview studio Next Level CEO client custom intro license purchased personal contribution

policies.title: ISTV offers documentary-style programming, not reality TV
question_families: Do we offer reality-TV casting opportunities?
Is the program a reality show or documentary style?
policies.decision: ISTV offers documentary-style programming, not reality TV.
policies.search_text: reality TV reality show documentary style programming casting opportunity format real estate

policies.title: Second calls are follow-ups and EOD corrections stay in the original thread
question_families: How are second calls classified in daily stats?
Where should I correct an inaccurate EOD stats submission?
policies.decision: Classify second calls as follow-ups in daily stats. If an EOD submission is incorrect, post the corrected stats in the thread of the original daily-stats submission. Boundaries: A live leaderboard RPC or RPCT correction is a Sales Tech action and is not completed by this answer alone.
policies.route_reason: Route only the live leaderboard correction action to Sales Tech.
policies.search_text: daily stats EOD second calls follow ups classify correction original submission thread leaderboard RPC RPCT Sales Tech Alex

policies.title: A direct Zoom link is allowed for the immediate booking case
question_families: May I send the Zoom link directly for an immediate outbound booking?
What should I do when an immediate booking cannot use my public calendar?
policies.decision: For an immediate outbound booking that cannot use the rep's public calendar, sending the direct Zoom link is allowed.
policies.search_text: immediate outbound booking public calendar cannot use send direct Zoom link fast track schedule

policies.title: The first no-answer text may include the rep's public OnceHub link
question_families: May I put my public OnceHub link in the first no-answer text?
Is the calendar link allowed in the first follow-up text when a lead does not answer?
policies.decision: A rep may include the rep's public OnceHub calendar link in the first text sent under the no-answer protocol.
policies.search_text: no answer protocol first text public OnceHub calendar link initial message include allowed

policies.title: Multiple partners from one business may appear in one episode
question_families: Can three business partners appear together in one episode?
Do partners from the same company need separate episodes?
policies.decision: Multiple partners from the same business may appear together in one episode. All participating partners should attend and be involved together in decisions; onboarding handles the execution details.
policies.route_reason: Use fulfillment only for execution details after the participation rule is answered.
policies.search_text: three multiple business partners same company one episode together separate episodes attend involved decisions onboarding

policies.title: A rep may call the same outbound lead twice in one day
question_families: Can I call the same outbound lead twice today?
Is a second outbound call to the same lead allowed on the same day?
policies.decision: Yes. A rep may call the same outbound lead twice in one day.
policies.search_text: call same outbound lead twice one day second attempt today allowed

policies.title: A rep may call an outbound lead displayed as assigned to Rudy Mawer
question_families: Can I call an outbound lead if Keap says Rudy Mawer has it?
Does a Rudy Mawer assignment prevent an outbound rep from calling the lead?
policies.decision: Yes. A rep may call an outbound lead whose Keap assignment displays Rudy Mawer.
policies.search_text: Keap assigned taken by Rudy Mawer outbound lead can call permission assignment

policies.title: Telephone payment and onboarding preserve Zoom video and recording
question_families: Can I take payment and onboard by phone when the client has no internet?
How should a phone onboarding call be recorded if the client cannot join Zoom?
policies.decision: When a client has no internet, payment and onboarding may be completed by phone, but keep the interaction on Zoom video when possible because that is company policy. An approved workaround is to keep Zoom running on the computer while calling the client by phone so Zoom records the conversation. Conditions: The client cannot join Zoom because internet is unavailable. Boundaries: This does not make phone-only handling the preferred default.
policies.search_text: no internet phone telephone payment onboarding Zoom video company policy recording computer call client workaround

policies.title: Wire closes require contract signature, proof, receipt confirmation, then PayMe posting
question_families: For a wire payment, can I sign the contract on Zoom and wait for confirmation before posting the sale?
What is the approved wire close sequence before PayMe posting?
policies.decision: For a wire payment, send the current contract on Zoom and have the client sign it. Obtain a screenshot or other proof of the wire as soon as possible, then check with Alex in 24 to 48 hours to confirm the wire was received. Do not post the sale in the PayMe channel until receipt is confirmed.
policies.route_reason: Route only the live wire-receipt confirmation or a wire exception; the reusable sequence is answerable.
policies.search_text: wire payment contract Zoom sign screenshot proof Alex 24 48 hours receipt confirmed PayMe sale posting approved sequence

policies.title: A rep may follow up after a pre-call cancellation unless the lead says do not contact
question_families: Can I follow up with a lead who canceled before the first call?
May I try to reschedule a lead who canceled before the call?
policies.decision: Yes. A rep may make a reasonable follow-up attempt after a lead cancels before the call to ask why and try to reschedule, as long as the lead has not said do not contact. Boundaries: This does not authorize contact after an explicit do-not-contact request.
policies.search_text: lead canceled before first call follow up ask why reschedule do not contact reasonable attempt

policies.title: Put the rep's name on the outbound sheet to mark a lead as taken and being contacted
question_families: How do I mark an outbound lead as taken on the dial sheet?
What should I enter on the outbound sheet when I am contacting the lead?
policies.decision: Put your name on the outbound sheet to indicate that the lead is taken and is already being contacted.
policies.search_text: outbound sheet dial sheet put rep name lead taken already contacted mark ownership

policies.title: Keep the completed greenlight in the original day's stats and reconnect for a follow-up
question_families: What should I do if the client's internet drops after I greenlight them?
Do I greenlight again after reconnecting with a client whose internet dropped?
policies.decision: If the client was already greenlit before the internet dropped, keep that greenlight in the original day's stats. Calling the client to reconnect and booking a follow-up is the correct next step. Boundaries: This does not decide an unasked or unresolved Call 1 versus Call 2 merge procedure.
policies.search_text: internet dropped after greenlight already greenlit original day stats reconnect booked follow up correct next step

policies.title: A bank closure does not create a Monday payment-deadline exception
question_families: Can a prospect pay Monday if the bank was closed and they missed the deadline?
Does bank closure create an exception to the Call 2 payment deadline?
policies.decision: A bank closure does not authorize a Monday exception after the prospect misses the applicable payment deadline. Under Rich's controlling normal main ISTV reapplication rule, the minimum wait is three months. Boundaries: A separately approved documented exception is a different decision and is not inferred from bank closure alone.
policies.search_text: bank closed closure payment deadline missed Sunday Monday exception no exception wait reapply three months main ISTV
